imports.module:
{
  getCategories,
  getModuleCategory,
  resolveModuleCategory,
  getModulesByCategory,
  sortModulesByInstallOrder,
} from './utils.js'

functions.name: shellQuote
functions.docstring: Shell-safe quoting using single quotes. Single quotes prevent all shell expansion except for the single quote character itself. To include a single quote: close the quote, add escaped quote, reopen quote. SECURITY: This is the only safe way to quote arbitrary strings for shell execution. @example shellQuote("hello world") → "'hello world'" shellQuote("it's") → "'it'\\''s'" (which produces: it's) shellQuote("$HOME") → "'$HOME'" (no expansion) shellQuote("$(rm -rf /)") → "'$(rm -rf /)'" (no command execution)

functions.name: shellQuoteVerifiedInstallerArg
functions.docstring: Quote verified-installer args. Most args are treated as literal words (single-quoted) to prevent injection. However, we allow specific runtime variables to be expanded: - TARGET_HOME - TARGET_USER SECURITY: - We do NOT use a blacklist (e.g. banning `$(`). - We use a strict tokenizer: allowed variables are wrapped in double quotes `"..."`, and EVERYTHING else is wrapped in single quotes `'...'`. - This ensures that input like `$(rm -rf /)` is treated as a literal string `'$(rm -rf /)'`.

functions.name: buildVerifiedInstallerPipe
functions.docstring: Build the pipe command from verified_installer.runner and args SECURITY: Uses shellQuote() to prevent command injection via args. Runner must be in ALLOWED_RUNNERS (enforced by schema, validated here too).

functions.name: getRunAsShellHelper
functions.docstring: Map module.run_as to the appropriate shell helper function name

functions.name: toHeredocDelimiter
functions.docstring: Generate a heredoc delimiter from module ID (sanitized, collision-resistant)

functions.name: toFunctionName
functions.docstring: Convert module ID to a valid bash function name

functions.name: toCheckId
functions.docstring: Convert module ID to a check ID for doctor Currently a passthrough - kept for future extensibility

functions.name: escapeBash
functions.docstring: Escape special characters for use inside double-quoted bash strings. Handles: backslash, double-quote, dollar sign, backtick

functions.name: encodeDoctorCommand
functions.docstring: Encode a doctor-check command into a single-line, tab-safe representation. Why: - We store checks as tab-delimited records in a bash array. - `read` consumes a single line, so raw newlines in commands break parsing. Encoding rules (decoded via `printf '%b'` at runtime): - Backslash -> \\ (preserves literal backslashes, prevents accidental escape decoding) - Tab -> \t  (keeps records parseable) - Newline -> \n (restores multi-line scripts when running the check)

functions.name: sanitizeForBashComment
functions.docstring: Sanitize a string for use in a bash comment. Replaces newlines and other control characters with spaces to prevent breaking the generated script structure.

functions.name: wrapInstallHeredoc
functions.docstring: Wrap install commands in a run_as_*_shell heredoc Uses single-quoted delimiter to prevent outer shell expansion

functions.name: generateInstallCommands
functions.docstring: Generate the install commands for a module Uses run_as_*_shell heredocs for proper user context execution

functions.name: generateVerifyCommands
functions.docstring: Generate verify commands for a module

functions.name: generateManifestIndex
functions.docstring: Generate manifest index script (data-only, deterministic)

functions.name: generateInternalChecksums
functions.docstring: Generate internal script checksums file (bd-3tpl). Computes SHA256 for critical internal scripts and emits a bash associative array.

functions.name: generateCategoryScript
functions.docstring: Generate a category install script

functions.name: generateDoctorChecks
functions.docstring: Generate doctor checks script

functions.name: generateMasterInstaller
functions.docstring: Generate master installer script

functions.name: escapeTs
functions.docstring: Escape a string for use inside a TypeScript string literal (double-quoted).

functions.name: formatTsArray
functions.docstring: Format a string array as a TypeScript literal, one item per line.

functions.name: getWebVisibleModules
functions.docstring: Get all web-visible modules, sorted by ID for deterministic output.

functions.name: generateWebTools
functions.docstring: Generate manifest-tools.ts — web tool data from manifest web metadata. Pure data file, no React imports, tree-shakable.

functions.name: generateWebCommands
functions.docstring: Generate manifest-commands.ts — CLI command references from manifest web metadata.

functions.name: generateWebTldr
functions.docstring: Generate manifest-tldr.ts — TL;DR card data from manifest web metadata. Focused subset for the TL;DR summary page.

functions.name: generateWebLessonsIndex
functions.docstring: Generate manifest-lessons-index.ts — mapping from module IDs to lesson slugs. Used to link module detail pages to onboarding lessons.

functions.name: generateWebIndex
functions.docstring: Generate manifest-web-index.ts — barrel re-export for all web generated data.

functions.name: showHelp
functions.docstring: Show help message
